content.rendered:
DCAT

The Data Catalog Vocabulary (DCAT) is an RDF vocabulary to describe datasets, data services and available resources in catalogs and portals. DCAT is a W3C Recommendation and an updated version (2) has been published in February 2020.

DCAT defines a set of classes and properties, and incorporates properties from existing vocabularies such as the Dublin Core vocabulary and FOAF. The figure below (taken from the W3C Recommendation) gives an overview of all the classes, properties and relations of the DCAT model:

Dgraph Types

Dgraph supports a type system that I want to use to encode the different DCAT classes and properties. To do so have to look into Dgraph’s different schema types:

  • Dgraph supports a set of scalar types, e.g. string, int, float, bool, dateTime
  • To further categorize nodes we can define own types; we will use this to define the types Dataset and Distribution.
  • There is no inheritance, no support of sub-properties and sub-types. This means we cannot define an abstract “dcat:Resouce” type as it is present in the DCAT model.
  • We cannot set the range of a property: In Dgraph we can use the uid type to denote a node-node edge, however, we cannot set the type of the target node.

Dgraph does not support namespaces. We encode this information by using the namespace as a prefix (separated by a period). The types in Dgraph are defined using a GraphQL-like syntax. Below you can see the type definitions for Dataset and Distribution; the full model can be found here.

type dcat.Dataset {
    dcat.distribution
    dcat.spatialResolutionInMeters
    dcat.temporalResolution
    dct.accrualPeriodicity
    dct.spatial
    dct.temporal
    prov.wasGeneratedBy
    # dcat:Resource
    dcat.contactPoint
    dcat.keyword
    dcat.landingPage
    dcat.qualifiedRelation
    dcat.theme
    dct.accessRights
    dct.conformsTo
    dct.creator
    dct.description
    dct.identifier
    dct.isReferencedBy
    dct.issued
    dct.language
    dct.modified
    dct.publisher
    dct.relation
    dct.rights
    dct.title
    dct.type
    odrl.hasPolicy
    prov.qualifiedAttribution
}

type dcat.Distribution {
    dcat.accessService
    dcat.accessURL
    dcat.byteSize
    dcat.compressFormat
    dcat.downloadURL
    dcat.mediaType
    dcat.packageFormat
    dcat.spatialResolutionInMeters
    dcat.temporalResolution
    dct.accessRights
    dct.conformsTo
    dct.description
    dct.format
    dct.issued
    dct.license
    dct.modified
    dct.rights
    dct.title
    odrl.hasPolicy
}

dcat.accessService: [uid] . # dcat:DataService
dcat.accessURL: string . # rdfs:Resource
dcat.byteSize: float .
dcat.compressFormat: string . # dct:MediaType
dcat.downloadURL: string . # rdfs:Resource
dcat.mediaType: string . # dct:MediaType
dcat.packageFormat: string . # dct:MediaType
dcat.distribution: [uid] . # dcat:Distribution
dct.accessRights: string . # dct:RightsStatement
dct.conformsTo: string . # dct:Standard
dcat.contactPoint: [uid] . # vcard:Kind
dct.creator: string . # foaf:Agent
dct.description: string @lang .
dct.title: string @lang .
dct.issued: dateTime .
dct.modified: dateTime .
dct.language: string . # dct:LinguisticSystem
dct.publisher: [uid] .
dct.identifier: string .
dcat.theme: string . # skos:Concept
dct.type: string . # rdfs:Class
dct.relation: string .
dcat.qualifiedRelation: string . # dcat:Relationship
dcat.keyword: string @lang .
dcat.landingPage: string . # foaf:Document
prov.qualifiedAttribution: string . # prov:Attribution
dct.license: string . # dct:LicenseDocument
dct.rights: string . # dct:RightsStatement
odrl.hasPolicy: string . # odrl:Policy
dct.isReferencedBy: string .
foaf.primaryTopic: string .
dct.accrualPeriodicity: string . # dct:Frequency
dct.spatial: string . # dct:Location
dcat.spatialResolutionInMeters: float .
dct.temporal: [uid] . # dct:PeriodOfTime
dcat.temporalResolution: string . # xsd:duration
prov.wasGeneratedBy: string . # prov:Activity
dct.format: string . # dct:MediaTypeOrExtent

Insert a DCAT Dataset

Let’s look at an example dataset from the European Data Portal using the DCAT vocabulary. The metadata for this dataset is available as RDF N-Triples:

<https://europeandataportal.eu/set/data/13347329-bundesamt-fur-statistik-bfs> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <http://www.w3.org/ns/dcat#Dataset> .
<https://europeandataportal.eu/set/data/13347329-bundesamt-fur-statistik-bfs> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/title> "Divorces by institutional units, duration of marriage and nationality (category) of both of the divorcing partners at divorce"@en .
<https://europeandataportal.eu/set/data/13347329-bundesamt-fur-statistik-bfs> <http://www.w3.org/ns/dcat#contactPoint> _:B0d9012c27d93aa9441eea1564897b62a .
<https://europeandataportal.eu/set/data/13347329-bundesamt-fur-statistik-bfs> <http://www.w3.org/ns/dcat#keyword> "bevolkerung"@de .
<https://europeandataportal.eu/set/data/13347329-bundesamt-fur-statistik-bfs> <http://www.w3.org/ns/dcat#keyword> "tabella"@it .
<https://europeandataportal.eu/set/data/13347329-bundesamt-fur-statistik-bfs> <http://www.w3.org/ns/dcat#distribution> <https://europeandataportal.eu/set/distribution/8273cee0-6700-443a-b201-bcb537ce70b1> .
<https://europeandataportal.eu/set/data/13347329-bundesamt-fur-statistik-bfs> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/issued> "2020-06-25T02:00:00"^^<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime> .
<https://europeandataportal.eu/set/distribution/8273cee0-6700-443a-b201-bcb537ce70b1> <http://www.w3.org/ns/dcat#accessURL> <https://www.bfs.admin.ch/asset/fr/px-x-0102020203_103> .
<https://europeandataportal.eu/set/distribution/8273cee0-6700-443a-b201-bcb537ce70b1> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <http://www.w3.org/ns/dcat#Distribution> .
<https://europeandataportal.eu/set/distribution/8273cee0-6700-443a-b201-bcb537ce70b1> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/title> ""@de .
<https://europeandataportal.eu/set/distribution/8273cee0-6700-443a-b201-bcb537ce70b1> <http://purl.org/dc/terms/title> ""@en .
<https://europeandataportal.eu/set/distribution/8273cee0-6700-443a-b201-bcb537ce70b1> <http://www.w3.org/ns/dcat#mediaType> "text/html" .

Dgraph does not directly support to load any RDF into the database. It creates a UID for each blank node used in the input. Also, it does not allow external IDs as node identifiers. The Dgraph documentation recommends to add an additional xid edge and store the external IDs and types as strings:

<0x321> <xid> "https://europeandataportal.eu/set/data/13347329-bundesamt-fur-statistik-bfs" .
<0x321> <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <0x123> .
<0x321> <dgraph.type> "Dataset" .

I also encoded the external IDs in the prefixes of the types and the properties, as you can see in the above schema. To insert the above RDF example, I have to simplify the input a bit:

  • Replacing all (external) subjects with newly introduced blank nodes
  • Adding a source edge to store these external links
  • Converting all remaining RDF object resources (<http://..>) to strings (“http://..”)

To insert triples in Dgraph we have to use the set keyword:

curl -H "Content-Type: application/rdf" -X POST localhost:8080/mutate?commitNow=true -d $'
{
  set {
_:dataset <source> "https://europeandataportal.eu/set/data/13347329-bundesamt-fur-statistik-bfs" .
_:dataset <dgraph.type> "dcat.Dataset" .
_:dataset <dct.spatial> "Kantone" .
_:dataset <dct.temporal> _:B89a1f708d00f5dad999618a00f47c9c3 .
_:dataset <dcat.keyword> "bevolkerung"@de .
_:dataset <dcat.keyword> "tabella"@it .
_:dataset <dcat.distribution> _:8273cee06700443ab201bcb537ce70b1 .
_:8273cee06700443ab201bcb537ce70b1 <dgraph.type> "dcat.Distribution" .
_:8273cee06700443ab201bcb537ce70b1 <dcat.accessURL> "https://www.bfs.admin.ch/asset/fr/px-x-0102020203_103" .
_:8273cee06700443ab201bcb537ce70b1 <dct.rights> "NonCommercialAllowed-CommercialWithPermission-ReferenceRequired" .
_:8273cee06700443ab201bcb537ce70b1 <dct.issued> "2020-06-25T02:00:00"^^<xs:dateTime> .
_:8273cee06700443ab201bcb537ce70b1 <dcat.mediaType> "text/html" .
 }
}

DCAT is a W3C Recommendation and an updated version (2) has been published in February 2020. DCAT defines a set of classes and properties, and incorporates properties from existing vocabularies such as the […]

content.rendered:
The CKAN software allows portal providers to include additional metadata fields in the metadata schema. When retrieving the metadata description of a dataset via the API, these keys are included in the resulting JSON under the key “extras”. However, it is not guaranteed that the DCAT conversion of the CKAN metadata contains these extra keys. Depending on the version and configuration of the DCAT export-extension there are three different cases:

1) Portal-specific mapping

The portal provider defines a mapping for certain CKAN fields to a specific RDF property.

For instance, data.gov.ie maps certain metadata keys to DCAT properties: the CKAN metadata description [json] for the dataset Modes of Travel in Dublin Region maps the keys theme-primary to the DCAT property dcat:theme (see the exported DCAT metadata [rdf]).

2) No mapping

Looking at the same dataset, we can see that there are other “extra” keys where no mapping to an RDF property exists, e.g., for the key collection-name. The metadata information will get lost if we only consider the exported DCAT.

3) Generic mapping by extension

Certain CKAN data portals map all available extra metadata keys by using the dct:relation (Dublin Core vocabulary) property. The key gets mapped to the rdfs:label property and the value to the rdf:value property, e.g., for the contact-email metadata key:

<http://example.com/example-dataset>
   dct:relation [
      rdfs:label "contact-email" ;
      rdf:value "example@email.com"
   ] ;

This generic mapping can be found on various data portals, for instance at data.gov.uk: e.g., the DCAT export [rdf] for the Road Safety Data contains all extra keys by using the dct:relation property.

A more detailed report can be found here.

title.rendered: Prototype implementation of ISWC 2016 paper is online

content.rendered:
The code of the prototype implementation of our paper at ISWC 2016 (“Multi-level semantic labelling of numerical values“) is online available:

https://github.com/sebneu/number_labelling

 

Please be aware that this implementation is only for demonstration purposes. The underlying background knowledge graph is based on 50 DBpedia properties, in detail described in the paper. This is a research project and we try to fix bugs and plan to extend the knowledge graph to other data sources.

The underlying background knowledge graph is based on 50 DBpedia properties, in detail described in the paper. This is a research project and we try to fix […]

content.rendered:
Here I started to collect URLs and APIs of existing CKAN instances. I came across the dataportals.org portal which provides a comprehensive list of Open Data sites and portals. However, there are just about 50 CKAN portals in this collection where some of them are down and a lot of APIs are missing.

Using a short script I harvested the dataportals.org CKAN portals and merge them with my list. Before adding new portals to the list I check if the URL (and the API) is accessible (performing an HTTP GET request).

The resulting portals are published on GitHub as CSV or as JSON data:
instances.csv
instances.json

However, there are just about 50 CKAN portals in this collection where some of them are down and a lot of APIs are missing. Using a short script I harvested the […]

content.rendered:
CKAN provides an Atom feed to get updates on edited and created datasets and resources. By default, the feed can be found at {portal-url}/revision/list?format=atom.

In the Python code example I accessed the feed using the feedparser package. The titles of the feed entries are of the following form:

r863bfa65-bfed-40b1-b6ee-e78275be3283 [ra2014:updated:resources]: REST API: Update Objekt ra2014

I used the information within the square brackets: ra2014 is the internal dataset name, updated tells you if the dataset is created or updated and the third value, resources, appears only if a resource of the dataset has been edited.

import datetime
import feedparser
FEED = 'https://www.data.gv.at/katalog/revision/list?format=atom'
def parse_feed(feed_url, days=30):
start_date = datetime.datetime.now() - datetime.timedelta(days=days)
d = feedparser.parse(feed_url)
total_ids = set()
created_ids = set()
updated_ids = set()
for post in d.entries:
date = datetime.datetime(*(post.updated_parsed[0:6]))
if date >= start_date:
t = post.title
s = find_between(t, '[', ']')
details = s.split(':')
if len(details) > 0:
id = details[0]
total_ids.add(id)
if len(details) > 1:
typ = details[1]
if typ == 'created':
created_ids.add(id)
elif typ == 'updated':
updated_ids.add(id)
print 'total number of edited datasets:', len(total_ids)
print 'number of created datasets:', len(created_ids)
print 'number of updated datasets:', len(updated_ids)
def find_between(s, first, last):
try:
start = s.index(first) + len(first)
end = s.index(last, start)
return s[start:end]
except ValueError:
return ""
if __name__ == '__main__':
parse_feed(FEED)
view raw ckan_revision_reader.py hosted with ❤ by GitHub

content.rendered:
The CKAN software is the de facto standard for Open Data portals. So I started to manually collect a list of portal URLs together with their API.

At the moment there is a focus on portals in Europe and especially on portals in Austria in the list. If you have any portals not listed (no matter which country) please contact me or leave them in the comments.

(Here is the link to the document on Google Docs)

I want to start a second list containing Socrata portals but I’m currently missing URLs. So I would be also interested in any Socrata portals!

UPDATE: I started to harvest dataportals.org and to publish an up-to-date list to GitHub. Here you can find more information.
